title: id::gen_id sequence wraps silently causing PRIMARY KEY collisions
description: src/id.rs: SEQUENCE is AtomicU16; after 65535 calls within the same millisecond it wraps to 0, producing an ID identical to a previous one in that millisecond. Both posts and delivery_queue use gen_id() as PRIMARY KEY TEXT, so the INSERT fails with a UNIQUE constraint error instead of succeeding. Under bursty fan-out to many followers this can happen in production. Fix: use AtomicU32 or AtomicU64, or check/reject on wrap.
close_reason: P0 fixes: AtomicU64, UTF-8 path, OsRng, 410 cleanup, constant-time key compare, checked_mul, SSRF guard, sanitized URLs, persona exists check, UTF-8 safe truncate
